intended_use: StrokeSENS ASPECTS is a computer-aided diagnosis (CADx) software device used to assist the clinician in the assessment and characterization of brain tissue abnormalities using CT image data. StrokeSEN
intended_use_full: StrokeSENS ASPECTS is a computer-aided diagnosis (CADx) software device used to assist the clinician in the assessment and characterization of brain tissue abnormalities using CT image data. StrokeSENS ASPECTS is indicated for evaluation of patients presenting for diagnostic imaging workup with known MCA or ICA occlusion, for evaluation of extent of disease. StrokeSENS ASPECTS provides information that may be useful in the characterization of ischemic brain tissue injury during image interpretation (within 12 hours from time last known well).
target_condition: Acute ischemic stroke with known MCA or ICA occlusion
ground_truth: Expert consensus reference standard (for standalone performance); ground truth ASPECTS score defined by expert neuroradiologist assessment (for MRMC and VCTA studies)
other_metrics: Standalone region-level Clustered ROC Analysis. MRMC reader improvement study: unaided AUC 68.6% vs aided 74.3% (5.7% improvement, p<0.001); unaided sensitivity 41.3% vs aided 51.0% (9.7% improvement, p<0.001); unaided specificity 95.9% vs aided 97.5% (1.6% improvement, p<0.001); Fleiss's Kappa inter-reader agreement unaided 32.3% vs aided 60.8% (28.5% improvement). VCTA verification: 97.0% concordance rate.
